_description: Dicebound species portrait rules. This file stores species-specific visual rules used by the backend portrait prompt system. It should only contain the official 2024 Player's Handbook core species supported by Dicebound. These rules focus on portrait anatomy, visible species markers, skin or scale handling, hair or head-detail handling, and negative prompt terms. Character-specific details such as selected scale colour, eye colour, hair colour, hair style, class, weapon and notable feature are added later by the prompt builder.

Aasimar.priority: Visually depict this Aasimar as a celestial-touched humanlike adventurer, not an elf, not a tiefling, not a fae and not a winged or horned being. The character must have rounded human ears, a humanlike face, normal humanoid anatomy, no pointed ears, no horns, no crown, no wing-like headpiece and no draconic or infernal features. Celestial presence should be shown only through subtle soft light, calm expression or gentle atmosphere.
baseLook: Subtle divine presence shown through soft lighting, calm expression or faint aura only
mustShow: Aasimar should look celestial rather than infernal, elven, tiefling or draconic
The ears must be rounded human ears. Do not make the ears pointed, long, elven or tiefling-like.
Rounded humanlike ears must be visible or implied, not pointed ears
The face should remain humanoid, natural and readable
The selected eye colour must remain accurate and visible
The selected skin tone must remain accurate and visible
The selected hair colour and hair style must remain accurate
Any celestial glow must be subtle and must not change the selected eye colour, skin tone or hair colour
mustNotShow: Large feathered wings unless specifically listed
Glowing orange eyes unless orange, amber or gold eyes are selected
Plain human with no celestial presence
Aasimar.skinInstruction: Use the listed skin tone as normal Aasimar skin. A very subtle celestial softness or faint aura is acceptable, but do not replace the listed skin tone with gold, silver, white, orange or glowing skin.
Aasimar.hairInstruction: Use the listed hair colour and hair style normally. Do not turn the hair into light, flames, metal, a halo, horns, a crown or glowing head ornaments.

Dragonborn.priority: Make the character clearly Dragonborn before anything else. Use wingless, bipedal draconic humanoid anatomy with a fully reptilian dragon head, visible scales and no human hair.
baseLook: Predator teeth or reptilian mouth structure
Visible scales across the whole face, snout, neck, shoulders, chest and upper torso
Humanoid posture with normal arms and normal shoulders
Clawed or talon-like hands if hands are visible
Horns, head ridges, spines, frills, crest or smooth scaled skull as the selected head detail
mustShow: The selected skin tone must be shown as the Dragonborn scale colour, not as smooth skin
The scale colour must cover the face, snout, neck, shoulders, chest and upper torso
The selected eye colour must be visible in the irises or eyes
The selected hair colour field must be interpreted as horn, crest, spine or head-detail colour, not human hair
The selected hair style field must be interpreted as Dragonborn head anatomy, not a mammalian hairstyle
If the head style is Smooth Scaled Head, show a smooth scaled skull with no hair, no crest, no frill and no crown of horns
If the head style is Crown Of Horns, show a crown-like arrangement of horns growing from the Dragonborn head
If the head style is Long Head Spines, show several long visible spines growing from the head
Dragonborn.skinInstruction: Use the listed skin tone as Dragonborn scale colour. The face, snout, neck, shoulders, chest and upper torso must all visibly use that scale colour. Do not turn scale colour into clothing colour, background colour or lighting.
Dragonborn.hairInstruction: Dragonborn do not have normal hair. Use the listed hair colour as horn, crest, spine or head-detail colour. Use the listed hair style as exact Dragonborn head anatomy, such as horns, crown of horns, crest ridges, head frill, bone crest, scaled crest, spines or smooth scaled head.

Dwarf.priority: Make the character clearly Dwarf. Use short, sturdy adult dwarf anatomy with compact strength.
mustShow: The character must look like a short adult, not a child
The body should look sturdy and compact
The selected skin tone, hair colour and hair style must remain visible
Dwarf.skinInstruction: Use the listed skin tone as normal Dwarf skin.
Dwarf.hairInstruction: Use the listed hair colour and hair style. Beards are optional, but they must not hide the face or selected notable feature unless the character data specifically supports them.

Elf.priority: Make the character clearly Elf. Use graceful elven humanoid anatomy with visible elven ears and refined features.
baseLook: Bright or unusual eyes are appropriate
mustShow: Pointed elven ears must be visible
The selected eye colour must remain visible
The selected hair colour and hair style must remain accurate
Elf.skinInstruction: Use the listed skin tone as smooth elven skin.
Elf.hairInstruction: Use the listed hair colour and hair style. Elf hair can be neat, long, braided or practical depending on the character sheet, but do not change the selected style.

Gnome.priority: Make the character clearly Gnome. Use small adult gnome anatomy with mature adult facial proportions and lively expression.
baseLook: Slightly sharp or quirky facial features
Adult body proportions at small size
mustShow: The character must look like a small adult, not a child
The selected eye colour must remain visible
Slightly pointed gnome ears should be visible
The selected hair colour and hair style must remain accurate
Gnome.skinInstruction: Use the listed skin tone as normal Gnome skin.
Gnome.hairInstruction: Use the listed hair colour and hair style. Slightly eccentric hair is acceptable only if it still matches the selected style.

Goliath.priority: Make the character clearly Goliath. Use very tall, powerful giant-blooded humanoid anatomy.
baseLook: Massive muscular or heavy build if selected
Stone-like, grey or earthy skin is appropriate if listed
Imposing presence without orc tusks or dragon scales
mustShow: The character should look larger and more imposing than a normal human
The selected build must be visible in the upper body
The selected skin tone must remain visible
mustNotShow: Thin delicate body unless the selected build is thin
Goliath.skinInstruction: Use the listed skin tone as Goliath skin. Grey, stone-like, pale grey, ashen or earthy tones are appropriate if listed, but do not replace the selected tone.
Goliath.hairInstruction: Use the listed hair colour and hair style. Bald, shaved, close-cropped or practical hair is appropriate if the character sheet supports it.

Halfling.priority: Make the character clearly Halfling. Use small adult halfling anatomy with rounded, grounded features.
baseLook: Broad or sturdy body if the selected build says broad or stocky
mustShow: The character must look like a small adult, not a child
The selected build must remain visible
The selected skin tone, hair colour and hair style must remain accurate
Halfling.skinInstruction: Use the listed skin tone as normal Halfling skin. Bronze skin should look warm and sun-touched if selected.
Halfling.hairInstruction: Use the listed hair colour and hair style normally.

Human.priority: Make the character clearly Human. Use natural human anatomy, rounded ears and ordinary human proportions.
mustShow: The character must look fully human
The selected skin tone must appear as normal human skin
The selected hair colour, hair style and eye colour must remain accurate
Human.skinInstruction: Use the listed skin tone as normal Human skin.
Human.hairInstruction: Use the listed hair colour and hair style normally.

Orc.priority: Make the character clearly Orc. Use large powerful orc anatomy with rugged orc features.
baseLook: Large visible tusks or lower fangs
Muscular or heavy body if selected
mustShow: Visible orc tusks or lower fangs
Strong or rugged orc facial structure
The selected skin tone must remain visible
The selected eye colour must remain visible
Orc.skinInstruction: Use the listed skin tone as Orc skin. Green, grey, ashen, bronze, deep brown or earthy tones are appropriate if listed, but do not replace the selected tone.
Orc.hairInstruction: Use the listed hair colour and hair style. Orc hair can look rough, shaved, braided or practical depending on the character sheet, but the selected style must remain visible.

Tiefling.priority: Make the character clearly Tiefling. Use infernal humanoid anatomy with visible horns and otherworldly features.
baseLook: Skin may be unnatural or fantasy coloured if listed
Hair arranged naturally around the horns
mustShow: Visible tiefling horns
Pointed tiefling ears
The selected skin tone must remain visible
The selected hair colour and hair style must fit around the horns
The selected eye colour must remain visible
mustNotShow: Plain human with no infernal features
Tiefling.skinInstruction: Use the listed skin tone as Tiefling skin. Unusual colours such as red, purple, blue-grey, ashen or pale tones are appropriate if listed, but do not replace the selected tone.
Tiefling.hairInstruction: Use the listed hair colour and hair style. Hair should fit naturally around the horns and must not hide the horns, ears or notable feature.
